description.content: Search and lookup endpoints for indexed devices.
Returns all services that have been found on the given host IP.
Returns the number of results a search would have, without consuming query credits or returning results.
Search Shodan using the same query syntax as the website. Uses one query credit per page after the first when filters are used.

response.name: Search results with matches and facets.

description.content: Returns the list of facets that can be used to summarize search results.
Returns the list of filters that can be used when searching.
Determine which filters and tokens make up a search query.
Request crawls of specific IPs, netblocks, or the entire Internet.
Returns the list of port numbers that Shodan is crawling on the Internet.
Returns the list of protocols that can be used when launching an on-demand Internet scan.

response.name: Map of protocol slug to human-readable name.

description.content: Request Shodan to crawl an IP or netblock. Each IP consumes one scan credit.

Create and manage alerts on monitored IP ranges.

Returns the network alerts that have been created on the account.
Returns the information about a specific network alert.
Edit the IP ranges or name of a network alert.
Remove the network alert and its triggers.

Disable a trigger on a network alert.

Browse and search saved Shodan queries.

Enterprise bulk data exports.
Returns the list of bulk data sets available to the account. Enterprise only.
Returns the list of files available within a bulk dataset. Enterprise only.
Forward, reverse, and domain DNS lookups.
Returns the subdomains and DNS entries known for the given domain.
Look up IP addresses for the given hostnames.
Look up hostnames for the given IP addresses.
Helper endpoints for HTTP headers and IP detection.
Returns the HTTP headers seen by the Shodan server.
Returns the IP address that the request was issued from.
Account, profile, and API plan information.
Returns information about the Shodan account.
Returns information about the API plan including remaining query and scan credits.
Enterprise organization management.
Returns information about the organization the account is part of.
Add a user to the organization.
Remove a user from the organization.

The Shodan REST API exposes search methods, host lookups, on-demand scanning, network alerts, notifiers, the saved-query directory, DNS lookups, utility methods, account/profile information, bulk data, and organization management for the Shodan search engine for Internet- connected devices.
